channel.title: Ancestors on Luke Jaeger
channel.description: Recent content in Ancestors on Luke Jaeger

item.title: Esther Ostrovsky Circa 1907
item.description:
Esther Ostrovsky was my second cousin once removed — my grandmother’s niece, though they were pretty close in age — and I’m happy to report that she was one of the ones who got out of Ukraine. Esther lived most of her life in and around Boston and died in 1984. One of my family’s only claims to fame was Esther’s son Elliot Silverstein, who directed a couple of Hollywood movies you might have heard of, and a bunch more you almost certainly haven’t, plus 4 episodes of the original Twilight Zone.

I have only two photographs of my old-country ancestors; this painting is based on one of them. Esther looks about 5 or 6 here. With her intense and piercing stare, she bears a haunting resemblance to my older daughter at the same age.

I used Photoshop to calculate the posterized color palette. Yes I’m still on my secondary-color kick: show orange, purple and green some love!

item.title: Extended Family | James Jamerson At Home
item.description: When I was 9 or 10, my mom took me to a survey of anamorphic art at the Brooklyn Museum. This exhibition spanned continents and centuries (from Holbein’s The Ambassadors to contemporary works) and it’s no exaggeration to say it blew my young mind permanently. Anamorphic and forced-perspective miniature environments have fascinated me ever since. Lately I’ve been designing and constructing them using CAD software, 3D printing, and laser cutting technologies.

item.title: Extended Family | Rabbi Nachman Of Breslav And Disciples Assembling New York Edison 60,000-KW Turbine Generator
Extended Family | Josephine Baker At IRT Substation 44, Bronx, 1927
Ancestors In 42nd Street Subway Concourse
item.description: I used this image on the postcards for my thesis exhibition. My uncle Sidney z"l got very offended. What can you do.
